root_text.an5.198:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgatā vācā subhāsitā hoti, no dubbhāsitā, anavajjā ca ananuvajjā ca viññūnaṁ.
root_text.an5.198:1.3: Kālena ca bhāsitā hoti, saccā ca bhāsitā hoti, saṇhā ca bhāsitā hoti, atthasaṁhitā ca bhāsitā hoti, mettacittena ca bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an5.198:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi aṅgehi samannāgatā vācā subhāsitā hoti, no dubbhāsitā, anavajjā ca ananuvajjā ca viññūnan”ti.

translation_text.an5.198:1.1: “Mendicants, speech that has five factors is well spoken, not poorly spoken. It’s blameless and is not criticized by sensible people.
translation_text.an5.198:1.3: It is speech that is timely, true, gentle, beneficial, and loving.
translation_text.an5.198:1.4: Speech with these five factors is well spoken, not poorly spoken. It’s blameless and is not criticized by sensible people.”

variant_text.an5.198:1.1: ananuvajjā ca viññūnaṁ → ananuvajjā viññūnaṁ (sya-all, km)
